name: clankers-self-validate
description: Self-validating development plugin — spawns a separate clankers instance to validate code changes, run tests, and review work

tool_definitions.name: validate
tool_definitions.description: Spawn a validator clankers instance to perform a custom validation task. The validator runs in a separate process with its own context and tools, and returns its findings. Use this for ad-hoc validation of any aspect of the current work.

task.description: The validation task to perform. Be specific about what to check, e.g. 'verify the changes in src/tools/mod.rs compile correctly and the new function signatures match the trait'

context.description: Additional context about what was changed or what to focus on. Optional but helps the validator understand scope.

cwd.description: Working directory for the validator (defaults to current directory)

agent.description: Agent definition to use for the validator instance

severity.description: How strict the validation should be. 'strict' fails on warnings, 'normal' is default, 'lenient' only fails on errors.

tool_definitions.name: validate_review
tool_definitions.description: Spawn a validator to review code changes. The validator will examine modified files (via git diff or explicit file list), check for bugs, style issues, correctness, and return a structured review.

files.description: Specific files to review. If empty, reviews all uncommitted changes.

focus.description: What to focus the review on, e.g. 'error handling', 'performance', 'API compatibility', 'security'

tool_definitions.name: validate_test
tool_definitions.description: Spawn a validator to run tests and report results. Can run specific test commands or auto-detect the test runner.

command.description: Specific test command to run, e.g. 'cargo test', 'npm test', 'pytest'. Auto-detected if not specified.

filter.description: Test filter/pattern to run a subset of tests, e.g. 'test_plugin' to only run plugin tests

fix.description: If true, the validator will attempt to suggest fixes for failing tests

tool_definitions.name: validate_check
tool_definitions.description: Run a quick validation check — compile check, lint, type check, or format check without running full tests.

checks.description: Which checks to run. Defaults to ['compile'].

tool_definitions.name: validate_tui
tool_definitions.description: Spawn clankers in a PTY, execute a sequence of TUI interactions (keystrokes, slash commands), and verify screen content against assertions. Use this to validate TUI features like panels, keybindings, rendering, and navigation.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this test validates

action.description: Action to perform. type is one of: type (type text), key (send named key: enter, esc, backtick, up, down, left, right, tab, backspace, space, ctrl+c, alt+enter), wait (wait ms), slash_command (enter insert, type command, submit)

wait_for.description: Text to wait for on screen after action

assert_visible.description: Text that must be on screen

assert_absent.description: Text that must NOT be on screen

tool_definitions.name: validate_visual
tool_definitions.description: Spawn a validator to visually inspect the terminal/TUI by taking a screenshot and analyzing the rendering. The validator captures the screen, examines the image, and reports any visual issues like layout corruption, misaligned elements, or rendering artifacts.

description.description: What the screen should look like — describe the expected visual state so the validator knows what to check for.

target.description: What to capture. 'screen' = full screen, 'window' = focused terminal window. Default: 'screen'.

checks.description: Specific visual checks to perform, e.g. ['no rendering corruption', 'panel visible on right side', 'status bar shows correct model']

delay.description: Seconds to wait before capturing (default 1). Lets the TUI settle.
